title: hydrangea macrophylla f. normalis
intro: Hydrangea macrophylla f. normalis, also known as the bigleaf hydrangea, is a type of flowering plant that is native to Japan and is highly valued for its medicinal properties and ornamental value. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and urinary tract infections due to its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The health benefits of hydrangea macrophylla f. normalis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting kidney function, making it a popular herbal remedy for those suffering from chronic health issues. Herbal preparations of hydrangea macrophylla f. normalis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the plant's roots, leaves, or flowers in hot water.
